names.scientific_name: Sodium aluminium phosphate (NaH14Al3(PO4)8·4H2O - acidic type; Na3H15Al2(PO4)8 - basic type)
names.japanese_name: リン酸アルミニウムナトリウム

natural_occurrence.description: Sodium aluminium phosphate does not occur naturally. It is synthetically manufactured for use in baking powder and processed cheese.

manufacturing.description: Produced by reacting aluminum hydroxide or aluminum sulfate with phosphoric acid and sodium carbonate or hydroxide. Two types exist: acidic SALP (for baking powder) and basic SALP (for processed cheese).

other_uses.household: Component of baking powder, self-raising flour.

eu.max_level_mg_kg: varies by category (typically 1000-40000 mg/kg depending on use)
eu.conditions: Permitted in baking powder, processed cheese, and specific food categories. Part of total aluminum exposure concerns.

usa.notes: Widely used in commercial baking powder and self-raising flour.

japan.japanese_regulation_name: リン酸アルミニウムナトリウム

safety_evaluations.jecfa_adi: not specified for SALP specifically (subject to aluminum limits)
safety_evaluations.efsa_adi: no ADI established (part of overall aluminum TWI of 1 mg/kg bw/week)

regulatory_history.event: EFSA re-evaluated aluminum-containing food additives and expressed concern about dietary aluminum exposure, particularly for children. Established weekly tolerable intake.

commonly_found_in: BAKING POWDER (major ingredient in many commercial formulations)
fun_facts: One of the most common leavening agents in COMMERCIAL BAKING POWDER - check your pantry!
Comes in two forms: 'slow-acting' (releases CO2 during baking) and 'double-acting' (releases some at mixing, more when heated)
Preferred over simple sodium bicarbonate in commercial baking because it doesn't leave a bitter aftertaste
The 'aluminum-free' baking powder trend is a response to concerns about aluminum exposure (though evidence is debated)
Has been used in baking for over 100 years with widespread use globally
Part of ongoing scientific debate about aluminum and health, particularly neurotoxicity concerns
Provides both leavening AND helps processed cheese stay smooth and melt evenly
